chart.noQuarterlyData: No quarterly data available for this period.
chart.pfcfAnnualOnlyTag: 4 annual years (max available for this ticker)
chart.pfcfNoData: Not enough history to compute P/FCF over this period.
chart.pfcfNegativeFcf: Shaded area: negative or too-weak FCF, P/FCF not meaningful.
chart.pfcfNoDataZone: Hatched area: no data over that span.
chart.insufficientHistory: Not enough history to plot a reliable trend.
chart.pfcfVerdictBelow: The latest multiple ({{latest}}×) is below the historical median ({{median}}×), attractive relative valuation.
chart.pfcfVerdictAbove: The latest multiple ({{latest}}×) is above the historical median ({{median}}×), stretched vs its own past.
chart.croceSub: Formula: FCF (SBC-adjusted) ÷ (Assets − CurLiab − Goodwill − Excess cash), Bettin/Mauboussin. Excess cash = max(0, cash − 2% × revenue).
chart.croceNoData: Not enough history to compute Cash ROCE over this period (needs positive FCF + positive capital employed over ≥ 4 quarters / 2 fiscal years).
chart.croceVerdictHigh: Cash ROCE durably above 15% ({{n}}/{{total}} periods), consistent operating value creation.
chart.croceVerdictMid: Cash ROCE above the threshold in {{n}}/{{total}} periods, average quality, watch closely.
chart.croceVerdictLow: Cash ROCE rarely above 15% ({{n}}/{{total}} periods), weak operating value creation.

analyse.h1Sub: Quality and resilience scores, followed by P/FCF valuation, in seconds.
analyse.resiliencePendingVerdict: The Resilience watch has not covered this company yet. Its score will appear automatically after processing.
analyse.qualCtaText: The qualitative analysis assesses the moat, revenue predictability and management quality, beyond the numbers alone.
analyse.managementCtaText: Assess capital allocation, governance, alignment and the leadership team's ability to execute.
analyse.resiliencePending: This company has not yet been covered by the Resilience watch. No points are deducted while data is pending.
analyse.resilienceNoEvidence: No source is available for this criterion.

resilienceScoreExplanations.review: The score remains under review because at least one material subtest is not sufficiently documented.
resilienceScoreExplanations.moat: {{score}}/3: {{wide}} independent wide moat(s) and {{narrow}} narrow moat(s); monopoly bottleneck: {{bottleneck}}.
resilienceScoreExplanations.disruption_resilience: {{score}}/3: across two disruptions, {{positive}} strengthen(s), {{neutral}} are neutral and {{negative}} weaken(s) the company.
resilienceScoreExplanations.residual_dependencies: {{score}}/2: {{total}} material dependence(s), including {{strong}} strongly mitigated, {{medium}} moderately mitigated and {{exposed}} exposed.
resilienceScoreExplanations.economic_persistence: {{score}}/2: future need {{need}}, the company's own role {{role}}, value capture {{capture}}, over a {{horizon}}-year horizon.
resilienceScoreExplanations.recurrence_balance: {{score}}/2: {{pillars}} of 2 pillars pass, revenue recurrence and balance-sheet shock absorption.

resilienceAudit.rentEvidenceQualified: Rent evidence accepted by the framework
resilienceAudit.independenceBasis: Evidence that the engine is independent
resilienceAudit.responseRelevance: Causal link between the response and the disruption
resilienceAudit.chinaCoreExposure: Share of core exposed to China
resilienceAudit.chinaLeverage: Effect of the Chinese ecosystem on the company
resilienceAudit.chinaMajorityPressure: Majority Chinese pressure forced by the framework
resilienceAudit.continuityImpact: Impact of the loss on core continuity
resilienceAudit.geographicAffectedCoreShare: Share of core affected by this shock
resilienceAudit.monetizationBasis: Evidence that the demand driver is monetized
resilienceAudit.futureNeedBasis: Positive basis for the future need
resilienceAudit.needEliminationEvidence: AI's effect on the end need
resilienceAudit.roleContinuityBasis: Basis for continuity of the role
resilienceAudit.roleDependsOnCurrentInterface: Role depends on the current interface
resilienceAudit.interfaceDependenceBasis: Dependence of the role on the current interface
resilienceAudit.interfaceCovered: Loss of the interface is covered by a transition
resilienceAudit.roleAbsorbableAcrossMajorityCoreWithinSe: Role absorbable across most of the core within 7 years
resilienceAudit.absorberClass: Actor that could absorb the role
resilienceAudit.continuityQualified: Role continuity qualifies under the framework
resilienceAudit.controlHeldByCompany: Control is economically held by the company
resilienceAudit.vendorSpecificControl: Control is specific to this company
resilienceAudit.controlSurvivesAiCommoditization: Control remains scarce after AI commoditization
resilienceAudit.controlReplaceableAcrossMajorityCoreWith: Replaceable across most of the future core within 5 years
resilienceAudit.durableFutureControl: Future control qualifies under the framework
resilienceAudit.essentialRepeatPurchases: Repurchase is essential to the economic need
resilienceAudit.wideInfrastructure: Wider threshold from a physical or regulatory moat
resilienceAudit.balancePillar: Balance sheet able to absorb a shock

values.essential_to_core: Repurchase is essential to the core need
values.core_impossible_over_24m: Core impossible for more than 24 months
values.material_impairment: Material impairment without loss of the core
values.role_survives_interface_loss: Role survives the loss of the interface
values.company_owned_or_exclusive_rights: Owned asset or durable exclusive rights
values.customer_or_third_party_controlled: Control held by the customer or a third party
values.scarcity_survives_ai_access: Scarcity survives broad access to AI
values.commoditized_with_ai_access: Control becomes commoditized with broad AI access
values.partial_only_or_over_5y: Partial substitution or more than 5 years
values.survives_other_engine_loss: Rent survives the loss of the other engine
values.no_majority_path_under_3y: No credible majority path within 3 years
values.quality_or_access_loss: Material loss of quality or access
values.participant_density_improves_outcomes: Density improves outcomes for other participants
values.usage_or_data_only: Usage or data without a proven network loop
values.material_cost_or_quality_loss: Material multi-homing cost or quality loss
values.company_controlled_fewer_than_3_substitu: Company-controlled bottleneck with fewer than 3 credible substitutes
values.not_company_controlled: Bottleneck not controlled by the company
values.material_dependencies_listed: All disclosed material dependencies are listed
values.reviewed_no_material_dependency: Sources reviewed; no material dependency identified
values.not_applicable_to_core_model: Category not applicable to the core business model
values.input_or_capacity_over_20: Over 20% of an input or capacity
values.single_source_core: Critical single source for the core
values.profit_or_fcf_over_10: Over 10% of profit or FCF exposed
values.five_year_cagr_over_5: Market growth above 5% over five years
values.profitable_core_cash_unproven: Profit proven, cash generation not established
values.dominant_share_current_plus_durable_barr: Current dominant share and durable barrier
values.outgrew_market_2_of_3: Above market in 2 of the last 3 years
values.matched_market_2_of_3: In line with market in 2 of the last 3 years
values.partial_only: Only tasks or the interface are partly eliminated
values.not_eliminated_by_scenario: Need affirmatively supported in the scenario
values.embedded_china_supply_chain: Long-standing integration with China's supply chain
values.non_core: No material impact on the core
values.platform_network: Human network is part of the service
values.trusted_brand_demand: Demand tied to a trusted brand
values.durable_brand_preference: Durable brand preference in an agent-led world

moat.label: Moat and differentiation
moat.measure: The depth, diversity and durability of the barriers protecting profits.
moat.scoreRule: 3 points for two independent wide engines or a monopoly bottleneck; 2 for one wide; 1 for at least one narrow; otherwise 0. A wide engine requires rent, hard replication and a substitution penalty.

disruption_resilience.label: Disruption resilience
disruption_resilience.measure: The model's ability to withstand technology shifts, AI and new competitors.
disruption_resilience.scoreRule: 3 points if at least one disruption strengthens the company and neither weakens it; 2 if both are neutral; 1 if exactly one weakens it; 0 if both do.

residual_dependencies.label: Residual dependencies
residual_dependencies.measure: The weight of suppliers, platforms, regulators, talent or human processes that remain critical.
residual_dependencies.scoreRule: 2 points when material dependencies are strongly mitigated; 1 with medium mitigation or one unmitigated exposure; 0 with two unmitigated exposures or an existential risk.

structural_demand_capture.label: Future demand capture
structural_demand_capture.measure: The ability to monetize structural demand already visible in revenue or contracts.
structural_demand_capture.scoreRule: 2 points when either a strong trend or essential demand is captured and monetized; 1 for two of trend, capture and monetization, or essential demand already monetized; otherwise 0.

economic_persistence.label: Role in tomorrow's economy
economic_persistence.measure: Whether the end need, the company's own role and its ability to capture value persist in a world where AI and automation are fully adopted.
economic_persistence.scoreRule: 2 points if the need, the company's role and value capture persist; 1 if the need persists together with either the role or value capture; 0 if the need disappears or the company becomes dispensable.

recurrence_balance.label: Recurrence and shock absorption
recurrence_balance.measure: Revenue recurrence and the financial capacity to absorb shocks without breaking the model.
recurrence_balance.scoreRule: One point for revenue recurrence and one point for a balance sheet able to absorb a shock, for a total of 0 to 2.

error.tooManyDesc: Temporary limit reached. Try again in a minute.
error.offlineDesc: Unable to reach the server. Try again in a moment.

netMargin.why: Out of €100 in sales, how much becomes profit once every expense is paid (production, wages, taxes, interest). It's the basic proof that the company makes money.
netMargin.how: Net income ÷ sales, over the last 12 months.

revenueGrowth5y.why: Is the company selling more and more, year after year? Without rising sales, its profits have little chance of growing.
revenueGrowth5y.how: Average sales growth over the last 5 years.

fcfGrowth5y.why: The cash that comes back to each share. A company can grow while creating new shares, which shrinks your slice. Here we measure what really comes back to you, share by share.
fcfGrowth5y.how: Growth of free cash per share over the last 5 years (free cash is the money left after paying for investments).

fcfMargin.why: Out of €100 in sales, how much turns into cash that's actually available, once investments are paid. This is the cash that can be paid back to you or reinvested.

operatingLeverage.why: Are sales growing faster than costs over the years? If so, margins widen (a favorable scissors effect) and the company gets more profitable as it grows; if costs rise faster, margins compress.
operatingLeverage.how: We track the operating margin (the share of sales left after running costs) over the last 5 years, to see whether the gap between sales and costs works in the company's favor.

cashRoce.why: For €100 invested in the business, how much cash the company generates each year. The higher it is, the more value it creates for shareholders; too low, it destroys value.
cashRoce.how: Free cash ÷ capital employed in the business (what the company has tied up to operate).

netDebtFcf.why: If the company put all its free cash toward repaying its debt, how many years would it take? The shorter, the less fragile it is in a rough patch.
netDebtFcf.how: (Debt − cash) ÷ annual free cash, which gives a number of years. No net debt means the company holds more cash than debt.

cashConversion.why: Does the reported profit actually turn into cash? If cash doesn't keep up over time, the profit is only on paper (customers who haven't paid yet, inventories piling up).
cashConversion.how: Free cash ÷ net income, as a %.

ccc.why: The number of days between when the company pays for its inventory and when its customers pay it. The shorter, the faster its cash turns over. Negative is ideal: customers finance the operations (like Amazon or Costco).
ccc.how: Days to get paid by customers + days of inventory − days to pay suppliers. We track the trend over the last 5 years.

pfcf.why: The price you pay today for €1 of the company's annual free cash. Even an excellent company becomes a bad buy if you pay too much for it.

valuation.why: Gives an indicative buy price to target a desired return: the right "entry timing." This criterion does NOT factor into the quality score, a good company stays good, it's just not yet at the right price.
valuation.how: Discounting of future cash (DCF) based on the target return, the growth assumption and the exit multiple.

shareCount5y.why: The share count decides the size of your slice. When the company buys back its shares, your slice grows; when it issues new ones (dilution), your slice shrinks.
shareCount5y.how: Change in the share count over the last 5 years. Falling = buybacks, rising = dilution.

valuation.note: Judged separately from the quality score. The recommended price is the entry point to aim for your target return.
valuation.unavailable: Valuation unavailable (FCF per share not computable for this stock).
valuation.sectorMedian: The median P/FCF of the “{{sector}}” sector is {{value}}×.

chart.approxNote: Cost of revenue is not published for this stock, DIO/DPO are computed via revenue (less precise in absolute terms, but the trend remains reliable).

items.q: How is the quality score computed?
items.a: On 10 objective financial criteria: profitability, sales growth, per-share earnings growth, expanding margins, return on invested capital, leverage under control, earnings-to-cash conversion, and collection cycle. Each criterion is validated (YES / PARTIAL / NO) against thresholds drawn from the financial literature (Buffett, Bettin-Mauboussin, Damodaran). The final score is the total.

Over 7,000 names tracked: NYSE & Nasdaq (US), Stoxx 600 and national European indices, major international markets. Free gives à-la-carte analysis on all of them; Pro unlocks full Europe and International data with complete quarterly histories.
No. Lubin Investment is a decision-support tool for retail investors. We do not provide personalized investment advice. Investment decisions remain your sole responsibility.

items.q: How do I cancel my subscription?
items.a: At any time from your account, one click. No commitment, no questions asked. Pro access stays active until the end of the current period.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), Apple Pay and Google Pay via Stripe. Secure payments, never stored on our servers.

items.q: Can I switch from monthly to yearly?
items.a: Yes, anytime from your account. The change is immediate with prorated adjustment.

hero.title: 10 criteria, public sources, zero black box.
hero.lede: Lubin Investment applies the same lens to every company: ten fundamental criteria, scored from each company's public official accounts. Here's exactly how the engine works.

why.title: Why this method
paragraphs: Most stock analyses rely on opinions, analyst consensus or momentum indicators. These signals change constantly and reflect mostly the market's mood. We chose to anchor on verifiable accounting facts, taken directly from the official accounts each company publishes.

The ten selected criteria cover three dimensions of a quality company: growth (revenue, FCF per share, margins), capital profitability (Cash ROCE, FCF margin) and balance-sheet strength (leverage, cash conversion, operating cycle). It's the same framework that the best long-term investors, Buffett, Mauboussin, Terry Smith, have used for decades, translated into reproducible formulas.

Each criterion answers a simple question, with a clear threshold. No opaque score, no secret weighting. If a company fails a criterion, we tell you which one and why. And if you disagree with one of our thresholds, you can knowingly ignore it.

criteres.pourquoi: A company that doesn't make money isn't an investment case. This first filter eliminates structurally loss-making companies.
Revenue growth remains the best driver of long-term value creation. We measure the 5-year trend to smooth out exceptional years.

criteres.formule: Cash per share growth over 5 years
criteres.pourquoi: What really matters to you as a shareholder is the cash generated per share. We strip out the shares handed to employees, which shrink your slice.

criteres.formule: Annual change in diluted share count over 5 years
criteres.pourquoi: Dilution is when a company issues new shares: your slice of the pie shrinks. The best companies do the opposite, buying back shares instead of issuing them.

criteres.formule: Cash margin = free cash flow / revenue
criteres.pourquoi: Free cash flow is the money actually left in the bank once everything is paid, far more reliable than accounting earnings. A high margin means each euro of revenue generates real cash.
Margins rising over the years reveal a real advantage: the company can raise prices or produce more cheaply. A sign of durable quality.

criteres.formule: Cash generated per €100 invested in the business (Cash ROCE)
criteres.pourquoi: How much cash the company generates for each euro actually invested in its business. Above 15 %, it puts its money to work very efficiently.
How many years of cash it would take to repay all the debt. Above 3 years, the risk becomes serious if things turn sour.
Checks that reported profits turn into real money, not just an accounting entry. A ratio durably below 1 is a warning sign.

criteres.formule: Days the company's cash stays tied up in its cycle (customers, inventory, suppliers)
criteres.pourquoi: The number of days cash is tied up between paying suppliers and getting paid by customers. Short or negative is excellent: its suppliers finance its growth (Apple, Amazon).

limites.title: Limits, what the method does not do

items.title: The method is retrospective.
items.text: It judges a company's historical quality. A quality compounder can perfectly well disappoint in the years to come, and a mediocre company can turn around.

items.title: It ignores valuation.
items.text: The 10 criteria say nothing about the price paid. An excellent company bought too expensive remains a bad investment. We separately publish valuation indicators (P/E, FCF yield, EV/EBITDA).

items.title: It's unsuitable for some sectors.
items.text: Banks, insurance, listed real estate (REITs), pre-profitability companies: net margin, Cash ROCE or CCC criteria don't apply as-is.

items.title: The data isn't perfect.
items.text: Some of it comes from reports the companies filled in poorly. We fix issues continuously, but residual errors can remain.

items.title: No method replaces your judgment.
items.text: Scores are a decision-support tool, not a buy recommendation. You remain the sole judge.

cta.title: Ready to apply the method?
cta.sub: Run an analysis on the stock of your choice. The 10 criteria, in plain language, in 30 seconds.

methodology.disclaimer: Lubin Investment provides a tool for the fundamental analysis of listed stocks, for informational and educational purposes. The scores displayed constitute neither investment advice nor a buy or sell recommendation. Past performance does not guarantee future returns. Do your own research and consult a licensed advisor before any investment decision.
